_comment: NEXUS I/O Driver Registry - Production Catalog v2.0.0

drivers.name: hmc5883l

initialization_sequence.desc: 8 samples averaged, 15 Hz output, normal measurement

selftest_procedure.action: Read ID registers A, B, C (0x0A-0x0C)
Set positive bias (reg0=0x71, reg1=0xA0, reg2=0x01)
Read X/Y/Z data
Set negative bias (reg0=0x72, reg1=0xA0, reg2=0x01)
Read X/Y/Z data
Restore normal config

known_limitations: Must not be exposed to magnetic fields > 3 Gauss or permanent damage may occur
Self-test consumes extra current and changes gain; normal config must be restored
Data may latch if I2C communication is interrupted mid-read; read all 6 bytes atomically
Temperature drift: +/- 0.3% per degree C from 25C
workarounds: Use set/reset pulse sequence (write 0x10 then 0x11 to reg0) after strong field exposure
If data latches, perform full re-initialization
Apply soft-iron calibration matrix stored in NVS

drivers.name: hmc5883l_l
drivers.description: HMC5883L low-noise variant with averaging and set/reset stabilization

mcu_requirements.notes: Same pinout as hmc5883l. Adds periodic set/reset for noise reduction.

initialization_sequence.desc: Gain +/-2.5 Ga for wider dynamic range
8 samples averaged, 30 Hz output, normal measurement

selftest_procedure.action: Read ID registers A, B, C
Run set/reset cycle and verify non-zero readings
Measure noise floor (50 samples, compute std dev)

known_limitations: Lower sample rate (30 Hz) due to 8-sample averaging
Set/reset cycle adds ~6ms latency to periodic reads
Same field exposure limit as standard HMC5883L
workarounds: Set/reset every N reads (configurable, default N=100)
Store calibration data per-device in NVS

drivers.name: ist8310
drivers.description: IST8310 3-axis magnetometer (TDK/InvenSense, lower power alternative to HMC5883L)

mcu_requirements.notes: Requires INT pin for DRDY; optional for polled operation.

selftest_procedure.action: Read WHO_AM_I (reg0x07)
Self-test: write 64 to reg27, wait 5ms, read data
Read 100 samples, check standard deviation

known_limitations: I2C address can be 14 (0x0E) or 12 (0x0C) depending on PCB strap
Must wait > 5ms after soft reset before any register access
Sensitivity drifts with temperature: ~0.05%/C
workarounds: Probe both addresses during init; accept first that responds
Apply temperature compensation from on-die temp sensor (reg28/29)

drivers.name: mpu6050

mcu_requirements.notes: AD0 pin high = 105 (0x69), low = 104 (0x68). INT pin recommended.

initialization_sequence.desc: Wake up, set internal clock to PLL with X gyro ref
Enable data ready interrupt on INT pin
INT pin active-low, open-drain, latched until cleared

selftest_procedure.action: Read WHO_AM_I (reg0x75)
Enable self-test: reg27=224, reg28=240, reg25=0
Read accel X/Y self-test (200 samples avg)
Read gyro self-test (200 samples avg, subtract idle)
Restore normal configuration

known_limitations: Gyroscope zero-rate offset (ZRO) can be +/- 20 dps; factory trim not always accurate
Accelerometer cross-axis sensitivity: ~2-3%
Temperature sensor accuracy is only +/- 3C; not suitable for precise measurement
I2C clock stretching not supported; keep SCL frequency below 400kHz
workarounds: Run gyro calibration (N samples at rest) and store offsets in NVS
Use complementary/Kalman filter for sensor fusion (see Reflex examples)
For magnetometer: chain with HMC5883L via auxiliary I2C passthrough

drivers.name: icm20948
drivers.description: TDK InvenSense ICM20948 9-axis IMU (accel + gyro + magnetometer)

mcu_requirements.notes: Uses I2C-to-SPI bridge to internal AK09916 magnetometer. User bank switching required.

initialization_sequence.desc: INT_PIN_CFG: latched INT, bypass enabled, clear on read

selftest_procedure.action: Read WHO_AM_I bank0 (reg0x00)
Access AK09916 via bypass, read WIA1/WIA2
Gyro self-test: bank2 reg03=28, reg04=128, reg01=4
Accel self-test: bank2 reg03=12, reg04=128, reg01=4
Mag self-test via AK09916 self-test (reg0x10=50), wait 1ms
Restore normal config

known_limitations: Bank switching adds complexity; I2C transactions must set bank before every register access
Magnetometer accessed via internal I2C bridge; max combined sample rate ~50 Hz
AK09916 magnetometer saturates above ~1500 uT
High power consumption; sleep mode recommended when not sampling
workarounds: Cache bank register and batch same-bank accesses
For higher mag rate, use SPI interface (ICM20948 supports 8MHz SPI)
Enable low-power mode between samples for battery-powered applications

drivers.name: vl53l0x
drivers.description: STMicroelectronics VL53L0X ToF laser distance sensor, 2-200cm

mcu_requirements.notes: Optional XSHUT pin for multi-sensor address reconfiguration.

initialization_sequence.desc: System interrupt config: new sample ready
Limit check enable: sigma and signal

selftest_procedure.action: Read model ID (reg192) and revision (reg194)
Measure distance to known target at 100mm
Measure distance to known target at 500mm
Cover sensor, verify sigma fail or max range

known_limitations: Effective range depends on target reflectance (white paper ~200cm, black felt ~50cm)
Crosstalk error at very short distances (<30mm); offset calibration needed
20ms typical ranging time limits max sample rate to ~50Hz
Multi-sensor: all share 0x29 default; requires XSHUT sequenced address change
workarounds: For multi-sensor: hold XSHUT low, release one at a time, assign 48-55 (0x30-0x37)
Run SPAD calibration during first boot; store in NVS
Use high-accuracy mode (increases ranging time to ~33ms but improves to +/-3%)

drivers.name: bme280
drivers.description: Bosch BME280 temperature, humidity, and pressure sensor

mcu_requirements.notes: Also supports SPI; see spi_device variant.

initialization_sequence.desc: CTRL_MEAS: temp oversampling x2, pressure x16, forced mode
CONFIG: IIR filter x16, standby 0.5ms (forced mode)

selftest_procedure.action: Read chip ID (reg208)
Read and verify trim parameters are non-zero
Take reading, verify temperature is in sane range
Verify pressure near sea level if altitude known

known_limitations: Forced mode requires explicit trigger for each reading (~6ms conversion time)
Humidity sensor response time ~8s (63% of step change)
Pressure accuracy degrades at high altitude without local barometric reference
workarounds: Use normal mode for continuous sampling (configurable standby time)
Apply altitude compensation using known local barometric pressure
For SPI operation, set CS low during register access

drivers.name: sht40
drivers.description: Sensirion SHT40-AD1B-R3 temperature and humidity sensor

mcu_requirements.notes: I2C clock stretching required. SHT40 stretches clock during measurement.

initialization_sequence.desc: Soft reset (command only, no data byte)

read_procedure.crc: CRC-8 of bytes[0..1] and bytes[3..4]; polynomial 0x31, init 0xFF

selftest_procedure.action: Read after soft reset, verify first sample valid
Read serial number (command 137)
Take 10 samples, verify std_dev < 0.2C and < 0.5%RH

known_limitations: I2C clock stretching is mandatory; some MCUs do not support it in HW
Max 1 MHz I2C but only 100 kHz recommended for noisy environments
Accuracy degrades near condensation (100% RH)
workarounds: Enable I2C clock stretching in driver (most HALs support it)
Heat the sensor briefly with heater command (48,50) to clear condensation
For ESP32: use I2C HW with I2C_ACK_FROM_ISR workaround

drivers.name: ina219
drivers.description: Texas Instruments INA219 shunt and bus voltage/current/power monitor

mcu_requirements.notes: External shunt resistor required; typical 0.1 ohm 1% 2W.

initialization_sequence.desc: Configuration: 16V bus, 320uV shunt, 12-bit ADC, continuous shunt+bus
Calibration register (must be set based on shunt value)
Wait for calibration to take effect

selftest_procedure.action: Read configuration register (reg0)
With known load, verify shunt voltage sign and magnitude
Verify bus voltage is near supply (no load)
Check power register matches V_bus * I_shunt

output_schema.description: Shunt voltage (mV), bus voltage (V), current (mA), power (mW)

known_limitations: Max bus voltage 26V (with 16V FSR) or 32V (with 32V FSR)
Shunt voltage range limited to +/- 320mV; larger shunt drops require external divider
Power register has limited resolution (1 LSB = 20 * current_lsb watts)
Accuracy depends heavily on shunt resistor tolerance and thermal EMF
workarounds: Store actual shunt resistance in NVS for per-board calibration
Use 4-wire Kelvin connection to shunt for best accuracy
For > 3.2A: use 0.01 ohm shunt with higher calibration value

drivers.name: as5600
drivers.description: ams OSRAM AS5600 magnetic rotary position encoder (12-bit, contactless)

mcu_requirements.notes: Also supports PWM output on DIR/OUT pin; can be read via GPIO pulse.

initialization_sequence.desc: OTP zero position (burn if needed, write 0 to use default)
OTP access: unlock (required before writing OTP)

conversion.status: reg11: bit3=magnet_present, bit4=AGC too low, bit5=AGC too high

selftest_procedure.action: Read status register (reg11)
Read AGC value (reg26)
Read magnitude (reg27, reg28)
Rotate magnet, verify angle changes through full range

known_limitations: Requires diametrically magnetized magnet at correct distance (0.5-3mm gap)
AGC auto-adjusts gain; if AGC saturates, readings become unreliable
End-of-life detection: if magnitude drops below threshold, magnet may be damaged
12-bit resolution = 0.0879 degrees per LSB
workarounds: Monitor AGC register and flag when outside 32..128 range
Store zero position offset in NVS after mechanical alignment
For higher resolution: average multiple consecutive readings

drivers.name: pca9685

mcu_requirements.notes: External oscillator input optional; default internal 25MHz.

initialization_sequence.desc: MODE1: normal mode, no auto-increment, no sleep
MODE1: restart (set bit7=1 to restore oscillator)
MODE2: totem-pole outputs, outputs change on ACK, invert=0

read_procedure.notes: PCA9685 is write-only for PWM values; read back is not supported.
read_procedure.read_fallback: Driver maintains shadow registers and returns last written values.

selftest_procedure.action: Read MODE1 register (reg0)
Write channel 0 to 50% duty, measure with oscilloscope
Set all channels OFF, verify outputs are low

output_schema.description: Shadow register readback of 16 PWM channel states

known_limitations: PWM frequency applies to ALL channels simultaneously
12-bit resolution: at 50Hz, one LSB = 0.0049ms (~4.9us)
Read-back of PWM registers is unreliable on some clones; shadow register required
Max output current per channel depends on V+ supply and external drivers
workarounds: For different frequencies: use two PCA9685 chips with independent PRE_SCALE
For servo: use 50Hz (PRE_SCALE=121 for 25MHz osc)
Chain up to 62 devices with All-Call address (112)

drivers.name: ms5611
drivers.description: TE Connectivity MS5611-01BA03 barometric pressure sensor

mcu_requirements.notes: Also supports SPI; see spi_device variant. Datasheet commands are 24-bit.

initialization_sequence.desc: Reset command (must be sent before reading PROM)

initialization_sequence.verify_crc: CRC4 of first 4 PROM words must match word 5 lower nibble

read_procedure.notes: D1=pressure ADC, D2=temperature ADC. OSR=4096 (cmd 72/88) takes ~9ms.

selftest_procedure.action: Read PROM CRC and verify
Read PROM coefficients; verify no word is 0 or 65535
Take temperature reading, verify reasonable
Take pressure reading, verify reasonable

known_limitations: ADC conversion time depends on oversampling ratio: OSR256=0.5ms, OSR4096=9ms
I2C address is set by CSB pin; 118=low, 119=high
First reading after reset may be inaccurate; discard it
workarounds: Discard first reading after init or reset
For altitude: use pressure formula alt = 44330*(1-(P/P0)^0.1903)
For SPI: pull CSB high for I2C mode, or use CS for SPI addressing

drivers.name: generic_adc
drivers.description: Generic ADC driver wrapping MCU internal ADC peripheral

mcu_requirements.notes: Requires at least one ADC channel. Number of channels depends on MCU.

initialization_sequence.action: Configure ADC channel with requested attenuation and bit width
Configure sample rate timer if continuous mode requested

read_procedure.notes: On ESP32, ADC2 is unavailable when WiFi is active.

selftest_procedure.action: Measure known voltage reference (if available)
Verify ADC calibration values are valid
Measure ground-connected channel

known_limitations: ESP32 ADC nonlinearity: up to +/- 6% uncalibrated
ESP32 ADC2 cannot be used when WiFi is active
Input impedance affects accuracy for high-impedance sources
Max sample rate varies by MCU and number of multiplexed channels
workarounds: Use esp_adc_cal_characterize() for ESP32 and store char curves
Add external buffer op-amp for high-impedance sources
Use ADC1 only when WiFi is needed
Average 4-16 samples for improved noise rejection

drivers.name: generic_pwm_servo
drivers.description: Generic PWM servo driver using MCU PWM peripheral

mcu_requirements.notes: Requires at least one LEDC/MCPWM timer. ESP32-S3 has 8 LEDC channels.

initialization_sequence.action: Configure PWM timer with requested frequency

read_procedure.notes: Read returns current duty cycle from shadow register.

selftest_procedure.action: Set 0% duty, verify output is low
Set 100% duty, verify output is high
Set 50% duty, measure with oscilloscope

output_schema.description: Current PWM duty percentage and pulse width

known_limitations: Max frequency depends on timer resolution and source clock
Servo standard: 50Hz, 1000-2000us pulse range
Shared timers limit number of independent frequencies
LEDC: max 13-bit resolution at 5kHz; resolution decreases as frequency increases
workarounds: For servos: use MCPWM for more precise timing than LEDC
For multi-frequency: allocate separate timers for each frequency group
Use DMA for smooth servo sweeps (ESP32-S3)

drivers.name: generic_digital_io

mcu_requirements.notes: Any GPIO pin capable of digital I/O.

selftest_procedure.action: For output: set HIGH, read back, expect HIGH
For output: set LOW, read back, expect LOW
For input: verify pull-up reads HIGH with no external connection

known_limitations: Input debouncing must be handled in software or external RC circuit
GPIO max current sourcing/sinking varies by MCU (typically 12-20mA per pin)
3.3V GPIO should not drive 5V peripherals without level shifter
Interrupt latency varies by MCU (typically 1-10us)
workarounds: Use gpio_set_direction for input with pull-up for open-drain detection
Add 100nF capacitor and 10K resistor for hardware debounce
Use bidirectional level shifter (BSS138) for 3.3V<->5V

drivers.name: generic_relay
drivers.description: Generic relay driver with safety interlock support

mcu_requirements.notes: Requires transistor/MOSFET driver between GPIO and relay coil.

initialization_sequence.action: Set GPIO to output, LOW (relay off = safe state)
Read NVS for last known state (if persistent)

conversion.notes: If kill-switch is active, write is rejected with NX_ERR_SAFETY_VIOLATION

selftest_procedure.action: Read back GPIO level, expect LOW (off)
Set ON, read back, expect HIGH
Set OFF, read back, expect LOW
Verify safety interlock rejects write during kill-switch

known_limitations: Mechanical relay switching time: 5-15ms; solid-state relay: <1ms
Relay contacts have limited lifetime (typically 100K-10M cycles depending on load)
Coil inductance causes flyback; external flyback diode required
No hardware feedback from relay contacts (use separate GPIO for monitoring)
workarounds: Use optoisolator for galvanic isolation
Add flyback diode (1N4007) across relay coil
Track cycle count in NVS for predictive maintenance
For solid-state relays: use MOSFET driver with zero-crossing detection
